verses.text: In a cave fleeing Saul, David shelters 'in the shadow of thy wings... until these calamities be overpast,' sure God will send from heaven and save. He hides in God. The safest refuge in danger is the covering of God's care.

In a cave fleeing Saul, David shelters 'in the shadow of thy wings... until these calamities be overpast,' sure God will send from heaven and save. He hides in God. The safest refuge in danger is the covering of God's care.

In a cave fleeing Saul, David shelters 'in the shadow of thy wings... until these calamities be overpast,' sure God will send from heaven and save. He hides in God. The safest refuge in danger is the covering of God's care.

His soul is 'among lions,' men with spears for teeth, yet he prays, 'Be thou exalted, O God, above the heavens.' Surrounded by fury, he seeks God's glory. The believer's first concern, even in peril, is that God be honoured.

His soul is 'among lions,' men with spears for teeth, yet he prays, 'Be thou exalted, O God, above the heavens.' Surrounded by fury, he seeks God's glory. The believer's first concern, even in peril, is that God be honoured.

Though they set a net for him, they fell into it themselves; 'My heart is fixed, O God... I will sing and give praise.' Faith is steady amid traps. A settled heart can sing even before the danger passes.

Though they set a net for him, they fell into it themselves; 'My heart is fixed, O God... I will sing and give praise.' Faith is steady amid traps. A settled heart can sing even before the danger passes.

'Awake, my glory,' he calls, rising early to praise God among the nations, 'for thy mercy is great unto the heavens.' Praise fills his soul. The persecuted believer turns his cave into a place of worship.
